title: fleet expand-deep --critique CLI flag (additive, byte-lock preserved)
description: Add a --critique boolean flag to the existing `fleet expand-deep <goal>` subcommand and thread it through runFleetExpandDeep into the decomposeGoalDeep call via a guarded spread, leaving the default no-flag path byte-identical to Phase 3 and the byte-locked command tree intact.
estimatedFiles: src/fleet/index.ts (additive: FleetExpandDeepOptions.critique?, registerFleetExpandDeepSubcommand .option('--critique'), runFleetExpandDeep guarded spread on the decompose call)
src/fleet/expand-deep.test.ts (edit) or src/fleet/expand-deep-critique.test.ts (new) — DI + command-tree + byte-identity tests

successCriteria.description: npm run build (tsc) is clean after the additive index.ts edits.
Strict typecheck passes; no explicit any; ESM .js imports; import type for type-only.
ESLint clean on the changed/new files.

runFleetExpandDeep(goal,{critique:true},deps) with no --yes writes a FleetManifestSchema-valid manifest to the resolved outPath and does NOT call the injected runFleet (write-and-stop preserved) — DI test.

Guarded-spread threading: with --critique the injected decompose receives critique:true; WITHOUT --critique the decompose arg object is byte-identical to Phase 3 (no critique key) and zero extra chat calls are emitted — asserted on the recorded arg object / call record.

--critique is exposed on the expand-deep subcommand alongside the existing 7 options; the byte-locked command tree is intact: `fleet <manifest>` positional + --concurrency/--root, the `fleet expand` subcommand, and the existing expand-deep options — asserted on the Commander tree.

On the --critique path: credential fail-fast (createClient first; missing key => no file, decompose never called), write-before-spawn (manifest exists on disk before runFleet), and --yes as the sole spawn gate are unchanged; with --yes runFleet is called exactly once with outPath AFTER the critic-reviewed manifest is written.

npm run build + full npm test pass; existing fleet suites (decomposer.test.ts, decomposer-deep.test.ts, expand.test.ts, expand-deep.test.ts, index.test.ts) remain green — no regression; git diff on index.ts shows zero deleted existing lines (purely additive).

generatorNotes: Read the architecture doc + ADR-2 (opt-in field / guarded spread) and ADR-5 (gate ordering) first. The ONLY behavioral change in runFleetExpandDeep is the decompose call (index.ts:324-325): `const decomposed = await decomposeDeepFn({ goal: goalWithHint, client, model, ...(opts.critique ? { critique: true } : {}) });` — the spread makes the arg object byte-identical when --critique is absent. Steps 1,3,4,5,6 (client build, assemble, atomic write, print, --yes gate; index.ts:304-386) are UNTOUCHED. Add `critique?: boolean` to FleetExpandDeepOptions (index.ts:261-276). In registerFleetExpandDeepSubcommand (index.ts:396-432) add exactly one `.option('--critique', 'Run a fresh-context critic gate that re-expands degenerate manifests')` beside the existing options and pass opts.critique into the action call — touch no existing option line. Do NOT add a sibling subcommand (LOCK2). decomposeGoalDeep already supports critique from Sprint 1; this sprint only wires the flag. Mirror the expand-deep.test.ts DI helpers (makeFakeDecompose/makeFakeClientBuilder/runFleetSpy). Commit only index.ts + the test file with 'bober(sprint-2): fleet expand-deep --critique flag, byte-lock preserved'.
evaluatorNotes: Independently verify: (1) `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD --name-only` lists ONLY src/fleet/index.ts and the test file; `git diff -- src/fleet/index.ts | grep '^-[^-]'` must be EMPTY (zero deleted lines — purely additive); decomposer-deep.ts, critic-deep.ts, decomposer.ts, manifest.ts, cli/index.ts NOT in the diff. (2) Byte-identity: a DI test must assert that WITHOUT --critique the decompose arg object has NO critique key and the recorded chat call count equals the Phase-3 baseline (zero extra). (3) Command-tree test asserts expand-deep now has a --critique flag AND `fleet <manifest>` positional + --concurrency/--root + the `expand` subcommand are still intact. (4) Spawn-safety unchanged: no --yes never calls runFleet; --yes calls it exactly once after the file exists; credential fail-fast writes no file. (5) Full npm test green, all five pre-existing fleet suites pass. Required strategies: build + typecheck (required), lint + unit-test.
